title: Visual curl
description: Given a vector field, is the curl at a certain point positive, negative, or zero?

assessment_items.question_content:
Here's a vector field:

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a019d6fc124cfc791376d74cd8e82a75c7ea6527)

**What is the sign of the curl at the center of the vector field?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The curl of a vector field measures how much the vector field rotates in a given region. Counterclockwise rotation means a positive curl, clockwise rotation means a negative curl, and no rotation means a zero curl. We can look at the vectors directly around a point to find its curl.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

All the vectors around the center point down, but the vectors on the left are bigger than the vectors on the right. We can think of this as the left vectors driving the rotation. Because the left vectors point counterclockwise in relation to the center, the rotation at the center is counterclockwise.
The curl at the center of the vector field is positive.

hints:
The curl of a vector field measures how much the vector field rotates in a given region. Counterclockwise rotation means a positive curl, clockwise rotation means a negative curl, and no rotation means a zero curl. We can look at the vectors directly around a point to find its curl.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

All the vectors around the center point to the right, but the vectors on the bottom are bigger than the vectors on the top. We can think of this as the bottom vectors driving the rotation. Because the bottom vectors point counterclockwise in relation to the center, the rotation at the center is counterclockwise.
The curl at the center of the vector field is positive.

The curl at the center of the vector field is positive.

The curl at the center of the vector field is negative.

hints:
The curl of a vector field measures how much the vector field rotates in a given region. Counterclockwise rotation means a positive curl, clockwise rotation means a negative curl, and no rotation means a zero curl. We can look at the vectors directly around a point to find its curl.

[[☃ explanation 1]]
All the vectors around the center point away from the top right with roughly the same magnitude on all sides. There's no rotation.
The curl at the center of the vector field is zero.
